ops.body: CLOSED AS SUPERSEDED (2026-07-19 session): the code this task targets no longer exists. cc_steer/retrain/tinker.py (train_lora :295-306, score_auc_tinker/score_rows_tinker :370-393) was deleted in commit 0bd4f00 "feat(retrain): watcher retrain on athome 0.6's pipelined engine" — the retrain lane now delegates all Tinker training + per-checkpoint scoring to athome.train.retrain ("pipelined fit with batched in-stream checkpoint eval" per the commit message; async forward_backward_async engine verified in the live athome tree at athome/train/engine.py:153), and cc-steer has since adopted athome 0.9.0 (41bf6a1, observe() envelope) with pyproject flooring experiment-at-home>=0.9.1. Fixes (1) and (2) are therefore implemented upstream by the migration; fix (3) checkpoint_fracs 4→3 is NOT applied — under the new architecture it is a checkpoint-selection-policy change (shrinks the candidate set feeding CheckpointPolicy/select and can change which adapter promotes), not a cost lever, and is dropped deliberately. Residual carried forward: criterion 3's steps/hour measurement — journal the next real retrain's rate against the 800-1330/hr band to confirm the athome engine actually delivers the pipelined wall-clock (folded into the E2E plan's next-retrain verification, not a standalone task). A workflow impl lane discovered the stale premise and stopped cleanly; no code was changed.
